note: myalgia + new-onset type 2 diabetes = labelled statin risks
haemorrhage / bleeding is the defining warfarin risk
well-tolerated antihistamine; mostly non-specific reactions. SOFT label.
trap — no FAERS records should exist

ADVERSARIAL (brand name). Lipitor IS atorvastatin, so the correct answer is 'signal'. But the tool searches openFDA generic_name only, so a brand name likely returns no data -> the agent may wrongly say 'insufficient-data'. This case is designed to EXPOSE the missing brand->generic normalization. A user asking about Lipitor deserves the atorvastatin answer.

CONTESTED. Generally well-tolerated, but carries a labelled risk of lactic acidosis (rare, serious). Tests whether the agent flags a rare-but-serious signal instead of defaulting to no-signal. SOFT label.
SIGNAL-IN-NOISE. GI bleeding / Reye's are genuine signals amid very high, noisy report volume. Tests signal-vs-noise discrimination at scale.
benign antihistamine; the correct answer is NOT signal. Tests whether the agent over-calls 'signal'. SOFT label.
